auth_notes: Most enterprise TMS platforms (like MercuryGate, Trimble, or BlueYonder) require a combination of an API Key, Client Secret, and a Tenant/Customer ID. Many legacy systems still utilize SOAP/XML authentication requiring specific header structures.
rate_limits: Varies by contract; typically 100 requests per minute with strict burst limits.
gotchas: Heavy reliance on XML/SOAP payloads in legacy versions requiring complex mapping
Strict IP whitelisting is often required for production API access
Asynchronous processing: API may return a 202 Accepted, requiring a polling mechanism for final status
client_must_provide: Static IP of the n8n instance for whitelisting
research_notes: Integration will require the HTTP Request node. Since many TMS providers use XML, the 'XML to JSON' and 'JSON to XML' nodes will be essential. Developers should implement a robust error-handling branch as TMS APIs often return 200 OK even when the business logic fails (errors contained within the body).

effort_recommendation.rationale: Integration will require the HTTP Request node. Since many TMS providers use XML, the 'XML to JSON' and 'JSON to XML' nodes will be essential. Developers should implement a robust error-handling branch as TMS APIs often return 200 OK even when the business logic fails (errors contained within the body).
